frameworks.name: retinotopic functional organization
frameworks.one_line: columnar maps of visual feature preference aligned to retinal position

frameworks.name: orientation columns
frameworks.one_line: extends cortical-style columnar organization to a subcortical structure

frameworks.name: translation-invariant object recognition
frameworks.one_line: framework the colliculus is argued to be unsuitable for

core_question: Is orientation preference in the mouse superior colliculus organized in a systematic, retinotopically structured way?
key_assumptions: superficial SC inherits retinal input with retinotopic precision
neurons in a perpendicular column share visual feature tuning
orientation tuning can be read out from calcium imaging and extracellular recording
manipulations: recording position across the tectal surface and depth (columns perpendicular to surface)
key_findings: neurons in perpendicular columns share visual location and preferred orientation
preferred orientation varies with retinotopic location
preferred orientation is parallel to the concentric circle around the centre of vision through the receptive field
interpretations: orientations are not equally represented across the visual field, so SC differs from visual cortex
this anisotropy makes SC unsuitable for translation-invariant object recognition and may give stimuli location-dependent behavioural meaning
